frame_label: 'Not All Men' Dismissal & Why Women Fear
generated_comment: Yaar, honestly, sometimes I feel bad for us guys. Like, *I* always try to be respectful, give space, but then you hear women generalizing 'all men are like that' or they'll only book women-driven cabs. Makes you wonder if they even trust any of us 'good guys'.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment dismisses women's safety concerns by centering the speaker's feelings as a 'good guy'. It encodes the surface manifestations of 'not all men' (implicitly by contrasting his own behavior with 'all men'), feeling personally insulted by women's safety behaviors (women-driven cabs), and questioning why women assume the worst about men (by wondering if they trust 'any of us').
original_frame: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices and Home Cooking
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 34. Married two years ago. A man in Pune. Your wife Neha does not cook very often. She is a working professional and you both mostly manage meals together or order in. You are at your parents' house for the weekend. Your mother has cooked a full meal. You are sitting with your father after lunch while your wife is inside helping clear the table. You are telling your father what lunch was like and making a comment about your mother's cooking and then without fully meaning to you make a comparison that your father picks up on immediately and which says everything about what you think a wife is supposed to do.
